stories.title: Setkání v kavárně
stories.description: Petr and Eva meet in a Prague café and exchange greetings and introductions.

paragraphs.czech: Petr a Eva sedí v kavárně v Praze.
paragraphs.english: Petr and Eva sit in a café in Prague.

sentences.czech: Petr a Eva sedí v kavárně v Praze.
sentences.english: Petr and Eva sit in a café in Prague.

paragraphs.czech: "Dobrý den," řekne Petr. "Jak se máte?"
paragraphs.english: "Good day," says Petr. "How are you?"

paragraphs.czech: "Dobře, děkuji. A vy?" odpovídá Eva. "Také dobře."
paragraphs.english: "Well, thank you. And you?" answers Eva. "Also well."

sentences.english: "Well, thank you. And you?" answers Eva.

paragraphs.czech: Petr řekne: "Jmenuji se Petr. A vy?"
paragraphs.english: Petr says: "My name is Petr. And you?"

paragraphs.czech: "Jmenuji se Eva," odpovídá. "Těší mě."
paragraphs.english: "My name is Eva," she answers. "Pleased to meet you."

paragraphs.czech: "Odkud jste?" ptá se Petr. "Jsem z Ameriky," říká Eva.
paragraphs.english: "Where are you from?" asks Petr. "I am from America," says Eva.

paragraphs.czech: "A já jsem z Anglie," říká Petr. "Je to hezké tady v Praze!"
paragraphs.english: "And I am from England," says Petr. "It is nice here in Prague!"

sentences.czech: "A já jsem z Anglie," říká Petr.
sentences.english: "And I am from England," says Petr.

vocabulary.notes: Location: v kavárně = in the café

stories.title: Oběd v restauraci
stories.description: Petr and Eva order lunch at a Prague restaurant. They ask the waiter for recommendations, discuss menu items, and handle payment.

paragraphs.czech: Petr a Eva jdou do restaurace. "Dobrý den. Máte stůl?" ptá se Petr.
paragraphs.english: Petr and Eva go to a restaurant. "Good day. Do you have a table?" asks Petr.

sentences.czech: Petr a Eva jdou do restaurace.
sentences.english: Petr and Eva go to a restaurant.

sentences.czech: "Dobrý den. Máte stůl?" ptá se Petr.
sentences.english: "Good day. Do you have a table?" asks Petr.

paragraphs.czech: "Ano, samozřejmě!" říká číšník. "Tady je menu."
paragraphs.english: "Yes, of course!" says the waiter. "Here is the menu."

paragraphs.czech: Petr čte menu. "Co doporučujete?" ptá se. Číšník říká: "Guláš je velmi dobrý!"
paragraphs.english: Petr reads the menu. "What do you recommend?" he asks. The waiter says: "The goulash is very good!"

sentences.english: The waiter says: "The goulash is very good!"

paragraphs.czech: "Já chci guláš," říká Petr. "A ty, Evo? Co chceš ty?"
paragraphs.english: "I want goulash," says Petr. "And you, Eva? What do you want?"

sentences.english: "And you, Eva? What do you want?"

paragraphs.czech: Eva říká: "Já chci rajskou polévku a kuřecí řízek." Číšník píše objednávku.
paragraphs.english: Eva says: "I want tomato soup and chicken cutlet." The waiter writes down the order.

sentences.czech: Eva říká: "Já chci rajskou polévku a kuřecí řízek."
sentences.english: Eva says: "I want tomato soup and chicken cutlet."
The waiter writes down the order.

paragraphs.czech: "Chcete pít něco?" ptá se číšník. "Ano, dvě piva, prosím," říká Petr.
paragraphs.english: "Do you want to drink something?" asks the waiter. "Yes, two beers, please," says Petr.

sentences.english: "Do you want to drink something?" asks the waiter.

paragraphs.czech: Jidlo přichází. "Mňam! Je to opravdu dobré!" říká Eva. Petr souhlasí: "Ano, je to vynikající!"
paragraphs.english: The food arrives. "Yum! It is really good!" says Eva. Petr agrees: "Yes, it is excellent!"

sentences.czech: "Mňam! Je to opravdu dobré!" říká Eva.
sentences.english: "Yum! It is really good!" says Eva.

paragraphs.czech: Po obědě Petr ptá: "Kolik to stojí?" Číšník říká: "Je to 450 korun." Petr dá spropitné.
paragraphs.english: After lunch, Petr asks: "How much does it cost?" The waiter says: "It is 450 crowns." Petr gives a tip.

sentences.czech: Po obědě Petr ptá: "Kolik to stojí?"
sentences.english: After lunch, Petr asks: "How much does it cost?"

stories.title: Cesta tramvají
stories.description: A tourist asks for directions to Wenceslas Square, buys a tram ticket, and travels through Prague's neighborhoods.

paragraphs.czech: Turista stojí na ulici v Praze. Není si jistý, kam jít. "Prosím, kde je Václavské náměstí?" ptá se.
paragraphs.english: A tourist stands on a street in Prague. He is not sure where to go. "Please, where is Wenceslas Square?" he asks.

sentences.czech: Turista stojí na ulici v Praze.
sentences.english: A tourist stands on a street in Prague.
He is not sure where to go.

sentences.czech: "Prosím, kde je Václavské náměstí?" ptá se.
sentences.english: "Please, where is Wenceslas Square?" he asks.

paragraphs.czech: Paní odpovídá: "Je to daleko. Jděte tramvají! Číslo 3 je správná linka."
paragraphs.english: A woman answers: "It is far. Go by tram! Number 3 is the correct line."

paragraphs.czech: "Kde koupím lístek?" ptá se turista. "V automatu tady," říká paní. "Jeden lístek stojí 30 korun."
paragraphs.english: "Where do I buy a ticket?" asks the tourist. "In the machine here," says the woman. "One ticket costs 30 crowns."

sentences.english: "Where do I buy a ticket?" asks the tourist.
"In the machine here," says the woman.

paragraphs.czech: Turista koupí lístek a čeká na tramvaj. "Tady přichází tramvaj číslo 3!" Sedá si do tramvaje.
paragraphs.english: The tourist buys a ticket and waits for the tram. "Here comes tram number 3!" He sits down in the tram.

sentences.czech: Turista koupí lístek a čeká na tramvaj.
sentences.english: The tourist buys a ticket and waits for the tram.
He sits down in the tram.

paragraphs.czech: V tramvaji je mnoho lidí. Turista vidí krásné budovy. "Wow, jak je Praha hezká!" říká.
paragraphs.english: There are many people in the tram. The tourist sees beautiful buildings. "Wow, how beautiful Prague is!" he says.

sentences.english: There are many people in the tram.
"Wow, how beautiful Prague is!" he says.

paragraphs.czech: Zástupce hlásá: "Václavské náměstí! Zde je Václavské náměstí!" Turista vstává a říká: "Děkuji!"
paragraphs.english: The conductor announces: "Wenceslas Square! Here is Wenceslas Square!" The tourist stands up and says: "Thank you!"

sentences.english: The tourist stands up and says: "Thank you!"

paragraphs.czech: Turista vylézá z tramvaje. Vidí Václavské náměstí - velké, krásné, plné lidí. "To je úžasné!" myslí si.
paragraphs.english: The tourist gets off the tram. He sees Wenceslas Square - large, beautiful, full of people. "That is amazing!" he thinks.

sentences.english: The tourist gets off the tram.

sentences.czech: Vidí Václavské náměstí - velké, krásné, plné lidí.
sentences.english: He sees Wenceslas Square - large, beautiful, full of people.

vocabulary.notes: Location: na ulici = on the street
Mode of transport: jet tramvají = to go by tram

stories.title: Přijetí v hotelu
stories.description: Petr and Eva arrive at a Prague hotel, check in, discuss their room, and ask about amenities and breakfast.

paragraphs.czech: Petr a Eva přicházejí do hotelu. Jsou unavení po dlouhé cestě. "Dobrý den! Máme rezervaci," říká Petr.
paragraphs.english: Petr and Eva arrive at a hotel. They are tired after a long journey. "Good day! We have a reservation," says Petr.

sentences.czech: Petr a Eva přicházejí do hotelu.
sentences.english: Petr and Eva arrive at a hotel.
They are tired after a long journey.
"Good day! We have a reservation," says Petr.

paragraphs.czech: Recepční se usmívá. "Samozřejmě! Na jaké jméno je rezervace?" Petr odpovídá: "Na jméno Petr Brown."
paragraphs.english: The receptionist smiles. "Of course! Under what name is the reservation?" Petr answers: "Under the name Petr Brown."

sentences.english: "Of course! Under what name is the reservation?"
Petr answers: "Under the name Petr Brown."

paragraphs.czech: Recepční píše v počítači. "Ano, tady je! Máte pokoj číslo 305 v třetím patře. Je to dvoulůžkový pokoj."
paragraphs.english: The receptionist types in the computer. "Yes, here it is! You have room number 305 on the third floor. It is a double room."

sentences.english: The receptionist types in the computer.
"You have room number 305 on the third floor."

paragraphs.czech: "To je perfektní!" říká Eva. "Má pokoj koupelnu?" Recepční odpovídá: "Ano, má vlastní koupelnu a sprchu."
paragraphs.english: "That is perfect!" says Eva. "Does the room have a bathroom?" The receptionist answers: "Yes, it has its own bathroom and shower."

sentences.czech: Recepční odpovídá: "Ano, má vlastní koupelnu a sprchu."
sentences.english: The receptionist answers: "Yes, it has its own bathroom and shower."

paragraphs.czech: "Jaký máte WiFi?" ptá se Petr. "WiFi je zdarma. Heslo je 'Hotel2024'," říká recepční.
paragraphs.english: "What WiFi do you have?" asks Petr. "WiFi is free. The password is 'Hotel2024'," says the receptionist.

sentences.english: "What WiFi do you have?" asks Petr.

paragraphs.czech: "Kdy je snídaně?" ptá se Eva. "Snídaně je od 6:30 do 10:00. Jídelna je v přízemí," odpovídá recepční.
paragraphs.english: "When is breakfast?" asks Eva. "Breakfast is from 6:30 to 10:00. The dining room is on the ground floor," answers the receptionist.

sentences.english: "The dining room is on the ground floor," answers the receptionist.

paragraphs.czech: Petr a Eva berou klíč. "Děkujeme! Takže pokoj 305," říká Petr. "Přesně! Výtah je tady. Vítáme vás!" říká recepční.
paragraphs.english: Petr and Eva take the key. "Thank you! So room 305," says Petr. "Exactly! The elevator is here. Welcome!" says the receptionist.

sentences.english: Petr and Eva take the key.

vocabulary.notes: Location: po cestě = after/during the trip
Location: v počítači = in the computer
Location: v patře = on the floor

stories.title: Rozhovor v kavárně
stories.description: Petr and Eva chat with a local Czech woman about their families, work, and interests while having coffee.

paragraphs.czech: Petr a Eva sedí v kavárně. Vedle nich sedí paní Petra, která je z Prahy. "Ahoj! Jsem Petra," říká.
paragraphs.english: Petr and Eva sit in a café. Next to them sits Mrs. Petra, who is from Prague. "Hi! I am Petra," she says.

sentences.czech: Petr a Eva sedí v kavárně.
sentences.english: Petr and Eva sit in a café.

sentences.czech: Vedle nich sedí paní Petra, která je z Prahy.
sentences.english: Next to them sits Mrs. Petra, who is from Prague.

paragraphs.czech: "Ahoj, Petro! Jmenuji se Petr a toto je Eva," říká Petr. "Potěšeny! Odkud jste?" ptá se Petra.
paragraphs.english: "Hi, Petra! My name is Petr and this is Eva," says Petr. "Nice to meet you! Where are you from?" asks Petra.

sentences.czech: "Jmenuji se Petr a toto je Eva," říká Petr.
sentences.english: "My name is Petr and this is Eva," says Petr.
"Nice to meet you! Where are you from?" asks Petra.

paragraphs.czech: "Jsem z Anglie a Eva je z Ameriky," odpovídá Petr. "Jak dlouho jste tady v Praze?" "Tři týdny," říká Eva.
paragraphs.english: "I am from England and Eva is from America," answers Petr. "How long are you here in Prague?" "Three weeks," says Eva.

sentences.czech: "Jsem z Anglie a Eva je z Ameriky," odpovídá Petr.
sentences.english: "I am from England and Eva is from America," answers Petr.
"How long are you here in Prague?"

paragraphs.czech: "To je krásné!" říká Petra. "Co děláte v Anglii?" "Jsem učitel," odpovídá Petr. "Učím angličtinu."
paragraphs.english: "That is wonderful!" says Petra. "What do you do in England?" "I am a teacher," answers Petr. "I teach English."

paragraphs.czech: "A já pracuji jako lékařka," říká Eva. "To je zajímavé!" říká Petra. "Máte rodinu?" "Ano, máme dvě děti," říká Petr.
paragraphs.english: "And I work as a doctor," says Eva. "That is interesting!" says Petra. "Do you have a family?" "Yes, we have two children," says Petr.

sentences.czech: "A já pracuji jako lékařka," říká Eva.
sentences.english: "And I work as a doctor," says Eva.
"Yes, we have two children," says Petr.

paragraphs.czech: "A já bydlím tady s mou matkou. Moje matka je důchodkyně," říká Petra. "Má matka je také důchodkyně," říká Petr.
paragraphs.english: "And I live here with my mother. My mother is retired," says Petra. "My mother is also retired," says Petr.

sentences.czech: "A já bydlím tady s mou matkou."
sentences.english: "And I live here with my mother."

sentences.czech: "Má matka je také důchodkyně," říká Petr.
sentences.english: "My mother is also retired," says Petr.

paragraphs.czech: "Co máte rádi? Jaké máte záliby?" ptá se Petra. "Já miluji fotbal," říká Petr. "A já čtu knihy," říká Eva.
paragraphs.english: "What do you like? What are your hobbies?" asks Petra. "I love football," says Petr. "And I read books," says Eva.

paragraphs.czech: "To je skvělé! Praha má krásné parky pro fotbal a krásné knihovny," říká Petra. "Chcete se sejít zase?" "Ano, s radostí!" říkají oba.
paragraphs.english: "That is great! Prague has beautiful parks for football and beautiful libraries," says Petra. "Do you want to meet again?" "Yes, with pleasure!" they both say.

sentences.czech: "Praha má krásné parky pro fotbal a krásné knihovny," říká Petra.
sentences.english: "Prague has beautiful parks for football and beautiful libraries," says Petra.

stories.title: Nouzový případ
stories.description: Petr suddenly feels ill while walking in Prague. He finds a pharmacy and doctor's office to get help.

paragraphs.czech: Petr jde po ulici v Praze. Náhle se necítí dobře. "Bolí mě hlava a břicho," říká si.
paragraphs.english: Petr walks down a street in Prague. Suddenly he feels unwell. "My head and stomach hurt," he says to himself.

sentences.czech: Petr jde po ulici v Praze.
sentences.english: Petr walks down a street in Prague.

sentences.czech: "Bolí mě hlava a břicho," říká si.
sentences.english: "My head and stomach hurt," he says to himself.

paragraphs.czech: Vidí lékárnu. "Pojď tam," myslí si. Vstupuje dovnitř. "Dobrý den! Potřebuji pomoc," říká.
paragraphs.english: He sees a pharmacy. "Let me go there," he thinks. He enters. "Good day! I need help," he says.

sentences.english: "Good day! I need help," he says.

paragraphs.czech: Lékárnice se ptá: "Co se stalo? Co Vám je?" Petr odpovídá: "Bolí mě hlava, břicho a cítím se slabý."
paragraphs.english: The pharmacist asks: "What happened? What is wrong with you?" Petr answers: "My head and stomach hurt and I feel weak."

sentences.czech: Petr odpovídá: "Bolí mě hlava, břicho a cítím se slabý."
sentences.english: Petr answers: "My head and stomach hurt and I feel weak."

paragraphs.czech: "To neznám," říká lékárnice. "Měl by jsi jít k doktorovi. Lékařská ordinace je vedle, o pět minut chůze."
paragraphs.english: "I am not sure," says the pharmacist. "You should go to the doctor. The doctor's office is nearby, five minutes walk."

sentences.english: "I am not sure," says the pharmacist.

sentences.czech: "Lékařská ordinace je vedle, o pět minut chůze."
sentences.english: "The doctor's office is nearby, five minutes walk."

paragraphs.czech: Petr jde k doktorovi. Recepční říká: "Počkejte prosím. Doktor bude za chvíli." Petr si sedá a čeká.
paragraphs.english: Petr goes to the doctor. The receptionist says: "Please wait. The doctor will be here soon." Petr sits down and waits.

paragraphs.czech: Doktor vyzývá Petra. "Tak, co Vám je?" ptá se. Petr vysvětluje: "Bolí mě hlava a břicho už tři dny."
paragraphs.english: The doctor calls Petr. "So, what is wrong with you?" he asks. Petr explains: "My head and stomach have been hurting for three days."

sentences.english: "So, what is wrong with you?" he asks.

sentences.czech: Petr vysvětluje: "Bolí mě hlava a břicho už tři dny."
sentences.english: Petr explains: "My head and stomach have been hurting for three days."

paragraphs.czech: Doktor ho vyšetří. "Máte chřipku," říká. "Musíte odpočívat a pít hodně tekutin. Tady je recept na léky."
paragraphs.english: The doctor examines him. "You have the flu," he says. "You must rest and drink plenty of fluids. Here is a prescription for medicine."

sentences.english: "You must rest and drink plenty of fluids."

paragraphs.czech: "Děkuji, doktore," říká Petr. "Kolik vás dlužím?" "Nic, je to zdarma v České republice," odpovídá doktor. "Budete brzy zdravý!"
paragraphs.english: "Thank you, doctor," says Petr. "How much do I owe you?" "Nothing, it is free in the Czech Republic," answers the doctor. "You will be well soon!"

sentences.czech: "Nic, je to zdarma v České republice," odpovídá doktor.
sentences.english: "Nothing, it is free in the Czech Republic," answers the doctor.

vocabulary.notes: Impersonal: bolí mě = it hurts me

stories.title: Nákupy v obchodě
stories.description: Eva shops for clothes at a department store in Prague. She browses, asks questions, tries items on, and negotiates price.

paragraphs.czech: Eva jde do obchodu s oblečením. "Vítáme vás!" říká prodavačka. "Hledáme něco pro sebe?" "Ano, hledám svetr," říká Eva.
paragraphs.english: Eva goes to a clothing store. "Welcome!" says the salesperson. "Are we looking for something for ourselves?" "Yes, I am looking for a sweater," says Eva.

sentences.czech: Eva jde do obchodu s oblečením.
sentences.english: Eva goes to a clothing store.
"Are we looking for something for ourselves?"
"Yes, I am looking for a sweater," says Eva.

paragraphs.czech: Prodavačka ukazuje svetry. "Máme tady krásné svetry různých barev. Jakou barvu chcete?"
paragraphs.english: The salesperson shows sweaters. "We have beautiful sweaters here in different colors. What color do you want?"

sentences.english: "We have beautiful sweaters here in different colors."

paragraphs.czech: "Mám ráda tmavomodrý. Jakou máte velikost?" ptá se Eva. "Máme XS, S, M, L, XL," odpovídá prodavačka. "Já mám velikost M," říká Eva.
paragraphs.english: "I like dark blue. What size do you have?" asks Eva. "We have XS, S, M, L, XL," answers the salesperson. "I wear size M," says Eva.

sentences.english: "What size do you have?" asks Eva.

sentences.czech: "Máme XS, S, M, L, XL," odpovídá prodavačka.
sentences.english: "We have XS, S, M, L, XL," answers the salesperson.

paragraphs.czech: Prodavačka dává tmavomodrý svetr vel. M. "Chcete si to vyzkoušet?" "Ano, kde je převlékárna?" "Tady, doleva," říká prodavačka.
paragraphs.english: The salesperson gives a dark blue sweater size M. "Do you want to try it on?" "Yes, where is the fitting room?" "Here, to the left," says the salesperson.

sentences.czech: Prodavačka dává tmavomodrý svetr vel. M.
sentences.english: The salesperson gives a dark blue sweater size M.
"Do you want to try it on?"
"Here, to the left," says the salesperson.

paragraphs.czech: Eva vychází z převlékárny. "Jak se mi to líbí?" ptá se. "Vypadáte úžasně! Barva Vám opravdu sluší," říká prodavačka.
paragraphs.english: Eva comes out of the fitting room. "How does it look on me?" she asks. "You look amazing! The color really suits you," says the salesperson.

sentences.english: Eva comes out of the fitting room.

sentences.czech: "Jak se mi to líbí?" ptá se.
sentences.english: "How does it look on me?" she asks.
"The color really suits you," says the salesperson.

paragraphs.czech: "Kolik to stojí?" ptá se Eva. "599 korun," odpovídá prodavačka. "To je drahé. Máte slevu?" "Dnes máme 20 procent slevu!"
paragraphs.english: "How much does it cost?" asks Eva. "599 crowns," answers the salesperson. "That is expensive. Do you have a discount?" "Today we have a 20 percent discount!"

sentences.english: "How much does it cost?" asks Eva.

paragraphs.czech: "Skvěle! Beru to," říká Eva. "Jak vám bude placeným? Kartou nebo hotovostí?" "Kartou, prosím," odpovídá Eva.
paragraphs.english: "Great! I will take it," says Eva. "How will you pay? By card or cash?" "By card, please," answers Eva.

sentences.english: "Great! I will take it," says Eva.

paragraphs.czech: Prodavačka zabalí svetr. "Tady je vaše nákup. Děkujeme a na viděnou!" "Děkuji! Na viděnou!" říká Eva a jde domů s novým svetrem.
paragraphs.english: The salesperson wraps the sweater. "Here is your purchase. Thank you and goodbye!" "Thank you! Goodbye!" says Eva and goes home with her new sweater.

sentences.czech: "Děkuji! Na viděnou!" říká Eva a jde domů s novým svetrem.
sentences.english: "Thank you! Goodbye!" says Eva and goes home with her new sweater.

vocabulary.english: goodbye (literally: until we see each other)

stories.title: Cesta do Nerudovy ulice
stories.description: Petr helps a lost tourist find Nerudova Street in the old town, giving detailed directions involving landmarks and compass directions.

paragraphs.czech: Petr jde po Staroměstském náměstí. Vidí turista, který vypadá zmatený. "Prosím, potřebuji pomoc. Hledám Nerudovu ulici," říká turista.
paragraphs.english: Petr walks across Old Town Square. He sees a tourist who looks confused. "Please, I need help. I am looking for Nerudova Street," says the tourist.

sentences.english: Petr walks across Old Town Square.
He sees a tourist who looks confused.
"I am looking for Nerudova Street," says the tourist.

paragraphs.czech: "Nerudova ulice? To je pěkné místo!" říká Petr. "Není to daleko. Můžu vás tam provést." Turista se usmívá. "Děkuji! To je velmi laskavé."
paragraphs.english: "Nerudova Street? That is a nice place!" says Petr. "It is not far. I can show you there." The tourist smiles. "Thank you! That is very kind."

sentences.czech: "Nerudova ulice? To je pěkné místo!" říká Petr.
sentences.english: "Nerudova Street? That is a nice place!" says Petr.

paragraphs.czech: Petr ukazuje cestu. "Vidíte ten chrám tamtam? Chrám sv. Mikuláše?" "Ano, vidím ho," odpovídá turista. "Jděte na sever, přímo k chrámu."
paragraphs.english: Petr shows the way. "Do you see that church over there? St. Nicholas Church?" "Yes, I see it," answers the tourist. "Go north, straight to the church."

sentences.english: "Do you see that church over there?"
"Yes, I see it," answers the tourist.

paragraphs.czech: "Když budete u chrámu, otočte se doprava. Uvidíte malou uličku. To je Nerudova," říká Petr. "Ulička jde strmě nahoru. Vidíte? Je to tam."
paragraphs.english: "When you are at the church, turn right. You will see a small street. That is Nerudova," says Petr. "The street goes steeply upward. Do you see? It is there."

sentences.czech: "Když budete u chrámu, otočte se doprava."
sentences.english: "When you are at the church, turn right."

paragraphs.czech: Turista se dívá směrem, kterým Petr ukazuje. "Ano! Vidím to! Děkuji!" "Není zač. Když se budete vracet, pojďte přes most. Most je na jihu, vedle nás."
paragraphs.english: The tourist looks in the direction Petr is pointing. "Yes! I see it! Thank you!" "You are welcome. When you return, go over the bridge. The bridge is to the south, next to us."

sentences.czech: Turista se dívá směrem, kterým Petr ukazuje.
sentences.english: The tourist looks in the direction Petr is pointing.

sentences.czech: "Když se budete vracet, pojďte přes most."
sentences.english: "When you return, go over the bridge."
"The bridge is to the south, next to us."

paragraphs.czech: "Chrámu je na západě od Nerudovy. Takže když budete na Nerudově a vraciíte se, půjdete na západ k chrámu a pak na jih k mostu."
paragraphs.english: "The church is to the west of Nerudova. So when you are on Nerudova and return, you will go west to the church and then south to the bridge."

sentences.english: "The church is to the west of Nerudova."

sentences.czech: "Takže když budete na Nerudově a vraciíte se, půjdete na západ k chrámu."
sentences.english: "So when you are on Nerudova and return, you will go west to the church."

paragraphs.czech: Turista si píše poznámky. "Sever - chrám - Nerudova. Západ - chrám. Jih - most. Rozumím!" "Skvěle! Užijte si Nerudovu ulici. Je to opravdu krásné."
paragraphs.english: The tourist writes notes. "North - church - Nerudova. West - church. South - bridge. I understand!" "Great! Enjoy Nerudova Street. It is really beautiful."

sentences.czech: "Sever - chrám - Nerudova. Západ - chrám. Jih - most. Rozumím!"
sentences.english: "North - church - Nerudova. West - church. South - bridge. I understand!"

paragraphs.czech: Turista si podává ruku. "Děkuji vám moc! Jste velmi laskavý." Petr se usmívá. "Hodně štěstí! Na viděnou!" Turista jde směrem k Nerudově ulici a Petr jde domů spokojený.
paragraphs.english: The tourist shakes his hand. "Thank you so much! You are very kind." Petr smiles. "Good luck! Goodbye!" The tourist goes toward Nerudova Street and Petr goes home satisfied.

sentences.czech: Turista jde směrem k Nerudově ulici a Petr jde domů spokojený.
sentences.english: The tourist goes toward Nerudova Street and Petr goes home satisfied.
